## 5. UX parity dashboard

Directional read of the redesign surface area, not a release claim.

| Area                        | State     | Maturity center   | Approx. UX parity | Current read                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| --------------------------- | --------- | ----------------- | ----------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| App shell                   | `done`    | 4 redesign-ready  | ~100%             | AppShell 5-zone grid + TopBar (project menu, mode pills, ⌘K, theme) + LeftRail (tree, keyboard nav) + Inspector (tablist, NumericField, quick-action buttons) + StatusBar (level, snap, grid, cursor, ws/save) all adopted in `/` route. View tabs (TabBar) with drag-reorder, localStorage persistence, per-tab viewport snapshots closed by T-05–T-07.    |
| 2D Plan canvas              | `partial` | 3 evidenced slice | ~75%              | PlanCanvas renders token-driven wires, snap, wall draw (T-01), theme refresh (T-02). Remaining: `planCanvasState.PlanCamera` / `SnapEngine` / `draftingPaintFor` not yet wired directly — canvas uses equivalent hand-rolled implementations (B01–B03 `partial`). Scale-dependent line weights and anchor-zoom not yet applied.                              |
| 3D Viewport                 | `done`    | 4 redesign-ready  | ~100%             | Viewport.tsx uses `cameraRig`, `walkMode`, `sectionBox`, `materials` (token-driven paint bundle), and `ViewCube`. Theme refresh wired (T-02). All B04–B08 modules adopted and evidenced.                                                                                                                                                                    |
| Tool palette / top ribbon   | `done`    | 4 redesign-ready  | ~100%             | `tools/toolRegistry.ts` + `ToolPalette.tsx` (`role="toolbar"`, ArrowLeft/Right, aria-pressed, dim-disabled, Tag submenu). All C02–C12 grammar contracts correct; wall draw end-to-end (T-01). Playwright baseline captured.                                                                                                                                 |
| Project Browser (left rail) | `done`    | 4 redesign-ready  | ~100%             | LeftRail.tsx: role="tree", sticky search with `focus-within:ring-2`, auto-expand, ArrowKeys/Enter/F2, `LeftRailCollapsed` strip. Project Browser rows dispatch by kind (level → plan, viewpoint → 3D, section/sheet/schedule). Playwright baseline captured.                                                                                                 |
| Inspector (right rail)      | `done`    | 4 redesign-ready  | ~100%             | Inspector.tsx: Properties/Constraints/Identity tablist, NumericField with `focus-within:ring-2`, dirty-state footer, empty-state quick-action buttons (W/D/M dispatch). InspectorContent discriminated-union renderers for every Element kind. Playwright baseline captured.                                                                                  |
| Status bar / footer         | `done`    | 4 redesign-ready  | ~100%             | StatusBar.tsx: level switcher, tool readout, snap role="switch" group, grid toggle, mono aria-live cursor coords, undo/redo, ws/save assertive pills. Wired in `/` route. Playwright baseline captured.                                                                                                                                                      |
| Command palette + shortcuts | `done`    | 4 redesign-ready  | ~100%             | RedesignedCommandPalette (cmdk, ⌘K, parseQuery + rankCandidates) + CheatsheetModal (`?`, section filter) both wired in RedesignedWorkspace. D01/D02/D03 all closed.                                                                                                                                                                                         |
| Theming                     | `done`    | 4 redesign-ready  | ~100%             | Full §9 token set, `state/theme.ts` cascade, Three.js material rebuild on toggle (T-02/V11), `@media (prefers-reduced-motion)` global transition override. Light+dark Playwright baselines captured.                                                                                                                                                         |
| Iconography                 | `done`    | 4 redesign-ready  | ~100%             | `@bim-ai/ui` registry (108 assertions), adopted across TopBar/Inspector/StatusBar/ToolPalette. No inline SVG in chrome.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| Seed house fixture          | `done`    | 4 redesign-ready  | ~100%             | V2 fixture covers all §27.1 counts; pytest + vitest evidence; V1 untouched.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| Accessibility               | `done`    | 4 redesign-ready  | ~100%             | Global `:focus-visible` ring (2 px `--color-ring`), global reduced-motion override, `focus-within:ring-2` on input containers (NumericField, search), aria-label on all interactive elements, live-regions assertive/polite per surface. A10 baseline contract vitest-evidenced.                                                                             |
| Motion                      | `done`    | 4 redesign-ready  | ~100%             | `design-systems/motion.ts` table (10-test vitest), `var(--motion-fast/base/slow)` tokens collapse to 0ms under `prefers-reduced-motion`. Global CSS override (`transition-duration: 0ms !important`) covers all Tailwind transitions. All chrome transitions ≤150ms (< 240ms ceiling).                                                                      |
| Onboarding / empty states   | `done`    | 4 redesign-ready  | ~100%             | `OnboardingTour` (5-step popover, spotlight, Esc/Enter/Arrow) + `UI_STATE_PATTERNS` (canvas-empty CTA button wired, network-offline, engine-error, conflict-409, permission-denied) both rendered in RedesignedWorkspace.                                                                                                                                    |

---

## 6. Recent Sprint Ledger

| Source                                                       | Scope                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               | Tracker effect                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |
| ------------------------------------------------------------ | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **T-08 / T-09 / WP-UI-F02 / fix(3d) wave** (`main`) | Four commits between the T-01–T-07 wave and the §28 sweep, each advancing a specific surface. (T-08, `4965a80d`) `PlanCameraHandle` imperative ref exposed from `PlanCanvas.tsx` — tab host snapshots `{ centerMm, halfMm }` on tab-switch and restores via `initialCamera` prop on remount, closing the §7C plan-camera-per-tab TODO from HANDOVER.md. (T-09, `4453dcde`) ViewCube upgraded from a flat 2D face-cross to a real CSS-3D cube: 80 × 80 px, `transform-style: preserve-3d`, full pose tracking (azimuth + elevation), drag-to-orbit via `window` pointermove/pointerup listeners feeding `onDrag(dx, dy)` → `rig.orbit()`, `suppressNextClick` guard so a drag does not fire an accidental face-pick; 6-test vitest updated with PointerEvent polyfill + `getByTestId("view-cube-stage")` target; closes V04 and V13 from §32. (WP-UI-F02, `846f56ef`) `ProjectMenu` gains an optional "Replay onboarding tour" item that calls `resetOnboarding()` + `setTourOpen(true)` in `RedesignedWorkspace` — satisfies the §24 requirement that the tour is resumable without a page reload; 2 new ProjectMenu vitest assertions (7 total). (fix(3d), `a1cfb4e7`) `--color-background` uses CSS Color Level 4 space-separated `hsl()` which Three.js's `Color.set()` silently discards, leaving the scene renderer background black in light mode. Fix: route token read through a temporary DOM element so the browser returns `rgb(r, g, b)` that Three.js can parse; closes V06 from §32. Validation: web typecheck clean; src vitest 1127 tests pass. | T-08 → closes plan-camera-per-tab TODO; wires part of WP-UI-B03 (B03 stays `partial` — full PlanCamera adoption pending). T-09 → closes V04 + V13 from §32; WP-UI-B06 evidence strengthened (already `done`). WP-UI-F02 replay → tour restart path documented and wired (F02 already `done`). fix(3d) → closes V06 from §32; WP-UI-A01/B07 token-parse robustness confirmed. |
| **§28 partial → done sweep** (`main`) | Closed 40 of 43 WP-UI-* rows to `done` (§4 done-rule satisfied). Code changes: (1) `workspace/TabBar.tsx` — added `aria-label` to the tab activate button; replaced `outline-none` with `rounded` to allow the global `:focus-visible` ring. (2) `workspace/Inspector.tsx` NumericField container — added `focus-within:ring-2 focus-within:ring-offset-0` so the compound input shows a 2 px `--color-ring` ring when the inner `<input>` is focused. (3) `workspace/LeftRail.tsx` search label — same `focus-within:ring-2` treatment. These three fixes satisfy the last remaining §4 criterion-8 (focus-visible on every interactive element) for A04, A05, A12. All other §4 criteria were already met: criterion 1 (layout) by V01–V15 closure; criterion 2 (tokens) by Phase A→F hex-to-token collapse; criterion 3 (icons) by lucide-only registry; criterion 4 (theme) by T-02 material rebuild; criterion 5 (keyboard) per component; criterion 6 (pointer) per snap grammar; criterion 7 (motion) by global `transition-duration: 0ms !important` reduced-motion override + all transitions ≤150ms; criterion 9 (evidence) by 1122 vitest + 7 Playwright baselines. Three rows remain `partial` (B01, B02, B03) pending direct wiring of `planCanvasState.PlanCamera` / `SnapEngine` / `draftingPaintFor` into `PlanCanvas.tsx`. §5 dashboard updated. OPEN_TASKS.md updated. Validation: web typecheck clean; src vitest 1122 tests pass. | WP-UI-A01/A02/A03/A04/A05/A06/A07/A08/A09/A10/A12 → `done`. WP-UI-B04/B05/B06/B07/B08 → `done`. input.description: Deep feature parity audit between old and redesigned workspace
input.prompt:
I'm doing a feature parity audit between the old BIM AI workspace and the redesigned workspace. The old design is at `/legacy` route (Workspace.tsx) and the redesign is at `/` route (RedesignedWorkspace.tsx). The user specifically mentioned "collisions" as something they don't see in the redesign.

Please search thoroughly for:

1. What features does `packages/web/src/workspace/Workspace.tsx` (the old workspace) have that might not be in `RedesignedWorkspace.tsx`? Look at both files fully.

2. Search for "collision" across the whole codebase — what collision detection/display exists? Is it surfaced in Workspace.tsx vs RedesignedWorkspace.tsx?

3. What does the state store (`packages/web/src/state/store.ts` or similar) expose that might not be wired into the redesign?

4. Look at `spec/revit-production-parity-workpackage-tracker.md` for any features that exist in the old system that are not yet in the redesign.

5. Look at `spec/prd/revit-production-parity-ai-agent-prd.md` for features described.

Give me a comprehensive list of features present in the old workspace or the engineering PRD that are NOT wired into the redesigned workspace. Be specific — file names, component names, state keys.

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive picture. Let me create the final report:

## Comprehensive Feature Parity Audit: Old Workspace vs Redesigned Workspace

Based on thorough analysis of both `/packages/web/src/Workspace.tsx` (legacy) and `/packages/web/src/workspace/RedesignedWorkspace.tsx` (redesign), here are the **features and state properties present in the old workspace that are NOT wired into the redesigned workspace**:

### 1. COLLISION DETECTION & HANDLING

**Feature:** Collaboration conflict queue (merge_id_collision detection)  
**Old workspace file:** `Workspace.tsx` lines 16-18, 185-186, 337-347, 383-391, 459-465, 1019-1071  
**Not in redesign:** No collaboration conflict queue (`collaborationConflictQueueV1`)  
**Impact:** Users don't see IFC merge/collaboration collision warnings when applying commands that conflict with other users' changes.

Related infrastructure:
- `/packages/web/src/lib/collaborationConflictQueue.ts` — exists but not used in redesign
- `/packages/web/src/lib/collaborationConflictStatus.ts` — formatCollaboration409Status() helper unused
- `/packages/web/src/lib/collaborationConflictQueue.test.ts` — tests `merge_id_collision` reason

---

### 2. INSPECTOR & AUTHORING WORKBENCHES (Major Gap)

**Old workspace has these specialized inspector panels that redesign does NOT:**

#### a) Room Separation Authoring
- **Component:** `RoomSeparationAuthoringWorkbench` (line 1812-1820)
- **File:** `/packages/web/src/workspace/RoomSeparationAuthoringWorkbench.tsx`
- **Function:** Allows drawing and editing room separation lines (boundaries for open-plan rooms)
- **Features:** Wired to `elementsById`, `violations`, `planProjectionPrimitives`, `onUpsertSemantic`

#### b) Level Datum Stack Workbench
- **Component:** `LevelDatumStackWorkbench` (line 1821-1825)
- **File:** `/packages/web/src/workspace/LevelDatumStackWorkbench.tsx`
- **Function:** Inspector for level elevation constraints and datum chains
- **Features:** Exposes `violations` readout, parent/offset level relationships

#### c) Roof Authoring Workbench
- **Component:** `RoofAuthoringWorkbench` (line 1826-1841)
- **File:** `/packages/web/src/workspace/RoofAuthoringWorkbench.tsx`
- **Function:** Semantic property editor for roof footprints, slopes, overhangs
- **Features:** Wired to `planProjectionPrimitives`, `revision`, `onPersistProperty`

#### d) Material Layer Stack Workbench
- **Component:** `MaterialLayerStackWorkbench` (line 1842-1847)
- **File:** `/packages/web/src/workspace/MaterialLayerStackWorkbench.tsx`
- **Function:** Editor for wall/floor/roof layer definitions and material assignment
- **Features:** Supports structured `wall_type`, `floor_type`, `roof_type` editing

#### e) Site Authoring Panel
- **Component:** `SiteAuthoringPanel` (line 1853-1858)
- **File:** `/packages/web/src/workspace/SiteAuthoringPanel.tsx`
- **Function:** Project settings, site boundary, topography, north arrow
- **Features:** Wired to `levels`, `onUpsertSemantic`

#### f) Room Color Scheme Panel
- **Component:** `RoomColorSchemePanel` (line 1860)
- **File:** `/packages/web/src/workspace/RoomColorSchemePanel.tsx`
- **Function:** Defines color schemes for rooms (by department, program, finish, etc.)
- **Features:** Manages `room_color_scheme` elements

---

### 3. ADVISOR & CODE VALIDATION

**Feature:** Advisor panel for design guidance and violations  
**Old workspace:** `AdvisorPanel` component (line 1862-1872)  
**File:** `/packages/web/src/advisor/AdvisorPanel.tsx`  
**State keys used (not in redesign):**
- `useBimStore((s) => s.buildingPreset)` — selected building/code preset
- `useBimStore((s) => s.setBuildingPreset)` — setter for preset
- `violations` — passed to advisor for filtering/explanation

**Not in redesign:** No advisor integration; code violations are not surfaced or actionable.

---

### 4. COMMAND-LINE INTERFACE & KEYBOARD HELPERS

**Feature:** Command Bar + Cheatsheet (in old, Cheatsheet exists in redesign but no CommandBar)  
**Old workspace components:**
- **CommandBar** (line 1981) — `/packages/web/src/cmd/CommandBar.tsx` — interactive command input
- **Cheatsheet** (line 1975-1979) — `/packages/web/src/cmd/Cheatsheet.tsx` — legacy hotkey reference modal

**Redesign has:**
- `CheatsheetModal` (line 630) — newer version
- **Missing:** `CommandBar` — no command-line semantic input in redesign

**Impact:** Users can't type commands like `:wall 0,0 10000,0` to semantic engine.

---

### 5. WELCOME & ONBOARDING

**Feature:** Welcome modal for first-time users  
**Old workspace:** `Welcome` component (line 1964-1973)  
**File:** `/packages/web/src/onboarding/Welcome.tsx`  
**Not in redesign:** No welcome modal; `OnboardingTour` exists but differs in scope

---

### 6. PROJECT BROWSER & EXPLORER PANELS

**Feature:** Project Browser (semantic model explorer)  
**Old workspace:** `ProjectBrowser` (line 1167-1171)  
**File:** `/packages/web/src/workspace/ProjectBrowser.tsx`  
**Not in redesign:** Replaced by `LeftRail` component (lines 704-758), but LeftRail has different information architecture

**Additional old panels NOT in redesign:**
- **Explorer panel** (line 1242-1269) — flat list of all semantic elements by kind
- **Issues & constraints panel** (line 1279-1301) — list of violations
- **Activity panel** (line 1303-1311) — revision/undo history per user
- **WS panel** (line 1271-1273) — WebSocket connection status

---

### 7. PLAN VIEW GRAPHICS & VISUALIZATION

**Feature:** Plan View Graphics Matrix  
**Old workspace:** `PlanViewGraphicsMatrix` (lines 55, 1636, 1673)  
**File:** `/packages/web/src/workspace/PlanViewGraphicsMatrix.tsx`  
**Function:** Table showing plan symbology decisions (template vs stored vs effective) for each category  
**Not in redesign:** No graphics matrix editor for line weights, patterns, colors per view

**Supporting components NOT in redesign:**
- **SavedViewTagGraphicsAuthoring** (lines 58-60, 1465-1478) — tag visibility/style editor
- **SavedViewTemplateGraphicsAuthoring** (lines 59-60, 1660-1672) — template graphics overrides

---

### 8. SHEET & SECTION RENDERING

**Feature:** Sheet Canvas (preview)  
**Old workspace:** `SheetCanvas` (line 888-894)  
**File:** `/packages/web/src/workspace/SheetCanvas.tsx`  
**Function:** Renders sheet layout with titleblock, placed views, viewports  
**Not in redesign:** No sheet canvas; Sheet mode shell exists but placeholder only

**Related:**
- **SectionPlaceholderPane** (lines 870-885) — section/elevation view placeholder
- Not in redesign: sections are mocked in `SectionModeShell` (line 949)

---

### 9. BROWSER RENDERING BUDGET & DIAGNOSTICS

**Feature:** Browser rendering budget readout  
**Old workspace:** (lines 291-300, 1072-1082)  
**Function:** Monitors memory/DOM/rendering performance of the plan canvas  
**Not in redesign:** No performance diagnostics exposed to user

**Related state (not used in redesign):**
- `scheduleBudgetHydration` — tracks active schedule tab row count
- `planProjectionPrimitives` — plan projection wire format (used in old for rendering)
- `planRoomSchemeWireReadout` — legend + program digest

---

### 10. PERSPECTIVE & LAYOUT CONTROLS

**State keys used in old but NOT in redesign:**
- `useBimStore((s) => s.workspaceLayoutPreset)` — layout preset selector (classic, split_plan_3d, split_plan_section, coordination, schedules_focus, agent_review)
- `useBimStore((s) => s.perspectiveId)` — discipline perspective (architecture, structure, mep, coordination, construction, agent)
- `useBimStore((s) => s.setWorkspaceLayoutPreset)` — setter
- `useBimStore((s) => s.setPerspectiveId)` — setter

**Impact:** Redesign has static "7-mode" system; old workspace has flexible layout+perspective matrix for different disciplines and viewing modes.

---

### 11. VIEWPOINT & 3D LAYER MANAGEMENT

**State keys used in old but NOT in redesign:**
- `useBimStore((s) => s.activeViewpointId)` — currently active saved 3D viewpoint (clip planes, layer visibility persist)
- `useBimStore((s) => s.activatePlanView)` — activates a saved plan view for clip/underlay
- `useBimStore((s) => s.setActiveViewpointId)` — setter
- `useBimStore((s) => s.viewerCategoryHidden)` — per-category visibility toggles (wall, floor, roof, stair, door, window, room)
- `useBimStore((s) => s.toggleViewerCategoryHidden)` — setter
- `useBimStore((s) => s.viewerClipElevMm)` — section box cap (clips above)
- `useBimStore((s) => s.viewerClipFloorElevMm)` — section box floor (clips below)
- `useBimStore((s) => s.setViewerClipElevMm)`, `setViewerClipFloorElevMm` — setters
- `useBimStore((s) => s.setOrthoSnapHold)` — orthogonal snap constraint during draw

**Old panel:** "3D layers" (lines 1173-1240) exposes these controls; redesign hides them.

---

### 12. COMMUNICATION & PRESENCE

**State keys used in old but NOT in redesign:**
- `useBimStore((s) => s.comments)` — UX comments on model/level
- `useBimStore((s) => s.mergeComment)`, `setComments`, `setIdentity` — setters
- `useBimStore((s) => s.presencePeers)`, `setPresencePeers` — collaborative presence
- `useBimStore((s) => s.userDisplayName)`, `userId`, `peerId` — identity

**Old panels:**
- "Comments" (lines 1896-1960) — comment thread UI
- User display name + peer avatar indicators (lines 987-995)

**Redesign:** No comments/collaboration UI exposed.

---

### 13. PLAN PRESENTATION & DETAIL LEVEL

**State keys used in old but NOT in redesign:**
- `useBimStore((s) => s.planPresentationPreset)` — visual style for plan (default, opening_focus, room_scheme)
- `useBimStore((s) => s.setPlanPresentationPreset)` — setter
- `useBimStore((s) => s.activePlanViewId)` — currently displayed plan view

**Old UI:** Plan style dropdown (lines 1110-1152) + stored in active plan view; redesign doesn't expose.

---

### 14. PLAN TOOL CONTEXT

**State keys used in old but NOT in redesign:**
- `useBimStore((s) => s.planHudMm)` — cursor position in plan (mm); used for precision feedback

**Old UI:** Status bar shows cursor coordinates; redesign uses tool label only.

---

### 15. AGENT REVIEW

**Feature:** Agent Review Pane  
**Old workspace:** `AgentReviewPane` (line 903)  
**File:** `/packages/web/src/workspace/AgentReviewPane.tsx`  
**Not in redesign:** `AgentReviewModeShell` exists (placeholder), but full pane not wired

---

### SUMMARY BY CATEGORY

| Feature Category | Old Workspace | Redesign | Gap |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Collaboration** | Conflict queue, presence, comments | None | Critical |
| **Advisor** | Full advisor panel with presets | None | Critical |
| **Authoring Workbenches** | 6 specialized panels (separation, datum, roof, material, site, color scheme) | None | Major |
| **Graphics & Symbology** | PlanViewGraphicsMatrix + tag/template editors | None | Major |
| **Sheet & Sections** | SheetCanvas, SectionPane | Mode shells only | Major |
| **3D Layers** | Category visibility, section box, viewpoint persistence | Hidden | High |
| **Views** | Layout presets (6) + perspectives (6) | 7 fixed modes | Medium |
| **Commands** | CommandBar for semantic input | Palette only | Medium |
| **Plan Tools** | Cursor HUD (mm), ortho snap | Minimal | Low |
| **Onboarding** | Welcome modal + tour | Tour only | Low |
| **Diagnostics** | Rendering budget, browser perf | None | Low |

---

### SPECIFIC ANSWER TO "COLLISIONS"

The user mentioned **"collisions"** specifically. This refers to:

1. **IFC Import Merge Collisions** (`merge_id_collision`):
   - Detected in `/packages/web/src/lib/collaborationConflictQueue.ts` 
   - Test coverage: `/packages/web/src/lib/collaborationConflictQueue.test.ts` line 2
   - Manifested in spec as `revit-production-parity-workpackage-tracker.md` lines 56, 107, 130, 258, 346
   - **The collision queue is built (line 343) but NOT displayed in redesign**

2. **Where collisions appear in old workspace:**
   - Status bar readout (lines 1019-1071): Full "Collaboration conflict queue (collaborationConflictQueue_v1)" section with:
     - `inspectionReadout` — primary message
     - `retryAdvice` — how to recover
     - `reason` — why conflict occurred (e.g., `merge_id_collision`)
     - `rows` — blocking rule IDs
     - `blockingCommandType` — which command step failed

3. **Not in redesign:**
   - No status bar (line 846-856 has minimal status)
   - No conflict detection display
   - Errors bubble up as generic errors in `seedError` state only (line 292)

---

### FILES INVOLVED (NOT WIRED IN REDESIGN)

- `/packages/web/src/lib/collaborationConflictQueue.ts` — collision detection
- `/packages/web/src/lib/collaborationConflictStatus.ts` — formatting
- `/packages/web/src/workspace/RoomSeparationAuthoringWorkbench.tsx`
- `/packages/web/src/workspace/LevelDatumStackWorkbench.tsx`
- `/packages/web/src/workspace/RoofAuthoringWorkbench.tsx`
- `/packages/web/src/workspace/MaterialLayerStackWorkbench.tsx`
- `/packages/web/src/workspace/SiteAuthoringPanel.tsx`
- `/packages/web/src/workspace/RoomColorSchemePanel.tsx`
- `/packages/web/src/advisor/AdvisorPanel.tsx`
- `/packages/web/src/workspace/ProjectBrowser.tsx`
- `/packages/web/src/workspace/PlanViewGraphicsMatrix.tsx`
- `/packages/web/src/workspace/SavedViewTagGraphicsAuthoring.tsx`
- `/packages/web/src/workspace/SavedViewTemplateGraphicsAuthoring.tsx`
- `/packages/web/src/workspace/SheetCanvas.tsx`
- `/packages/web/src/workspace/SectionPlaceholderPane.tsx`
- `/packages/web/src/workspace/browserRenderingBudgetReadout.ts`
- `/packages/web/src/cmd/CommandBar.tsx`
- `/packages/web/src/onboarding/Welcome.tsx`

**Not in redesign:** No performance diagnostics exposed to user

- `useBimStore((s) => s.perspectiveId)` — discipline perspective (architecture, structure, mep, coordination, construction, agent)
- `useBimStore((s) => s.setWorkspaceLayoutPreset)` — setter
- `useBimStore((s) => s.setPerspectiveId)` — setter

---

- `useBimStore((s) => s.setActiveViewpointId)` — setter

- `useBimStore((s) => s.viewerClipElevMm)` — section box cap (clips above)
- `useBimStore((s) => s.viewerClipFloorElevMm)` — section box floor (clips below)

- `useBimStore((s) => s.setOrthoSnapHold)` — orthogonal snap constraint during draw

aiTitle: Review handover and open tasks documentation

content.text:
Here's where things actually stand — a frank assessment rather than the optimistic dashboard.

---

## Feature Parity: Old vs. Redesigned Workspace

The §5 dashboard reads ~100% across most areas but that's measuring the *chrome redesign* only — it says "did we redesign the UI shell?" not "did we port every feature the old workspace had?" Several significant old-workspace capabilities are completely absent from the redesign.

---

### Collisions — your specific example

You're right. The collision detection exists but is dark in the redesign.

**What's built:** `packages/web/src/lib/collaborationConflictQueue.ts` detects `merge_id_collision` events from the WebSocket — when two users' edits conflict during an IFC merge, this queue captures the blocking command, the reason, the retry advice.

**What's missing:** The old `Workspace.tsx` had a full status-bar section (lines 1019–1071) that rendered `inspectionReadout`, `retryAdvice`, `reason`, and `rows` from the conflict queue. `RedesignedWorkspace.tsx` uses none of this. Conflicts surface only as a generic `seedError`, losing all actionable information.

---

### Full gap table

| Feature | Old Workspace | Redesign | Severity |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Collaboration conflict queue** (merge_id_collision detection + readout) | Full status panel | Ignored | **Critical** |
| **Advisor panel** (`AdvisorPanel.tsx`, building preset, violation filtering) | Wired | `AgentReviewModeShell` is a placeholder | **Critical** |
| **Authoring workbenches** (6 panels: room separation, level datum, roof, material layer stack, site, room color scheme) | All wired | None | **Major** |
| **Plan view graphics matrix** (`PlanViewGraphicsMatrix.tsx` — per-category line weights, patterns, colors per view) | Wired | None | **Major** |
| **3D layer visibility** (per-category toggle: wall/floor/roof/stair/door/window/room) | Full panel | Hidden — state exists, no UI | **Major** |
| **Sheet canvas** (`SheetCanvas.tsx` — titleblock, placed views, viewports) | Wired | Mode shell placeholder only | **Major** |
| **Saved view graphics** (tag + template graphics overrides per view) | Wired | None | **Major** |
| **Collaboration presence** (peers, user identity, comments) | Comments panel + avatars | None | **High** |
| **Active viewpoint persistence** (saved 3D viewpoints with clip planes + layer visibility) | Wired | Not wired | **High** |
| **Section box clip elevation** (`viewerClipElevMm`, `viewerClipFloorElevMm`) | Exposed in UI | State exists, no UI | **High** |
| **Layout presets** (6 workspace layouts: classic, split_plan_3d, coordination, schedules_focus, etc.) | Selector in topbar | 7 fixed modes | **Medium** |
| **Perspective/discipline** (architecture, structure, MEP, coordination, construction) | Selector | None | **Medium** |
| **Plan presentation preset** (default, opening_focus, room_scheme) | Dropdown | None | **Medium** |
| **CommandBar** (semantic command-line input `:wall 0,0 10000,0`) | Present | RedesignedCommandPalette replaces but doesn't cover raw command input | **Medium** |
| **Agent Review pane** (full `AgentReviewPane.tsx`) | Wired | Mode shell placeholder | **Medium** |
| **Welcome modal** | First-run modal | OnboardingTour only | Low |
| **Rendering budget readout** | Browser perf panel | None | Low |

---

### Why §5 doesn't show this

The §5 dashboard and §28 WP-UI table were scoped to the *redesign work packages* — did the new chrome components get built and evidenced? They were. But those work packages never asked "did we carry over every feature from `Workspace.tsx`?" — they only asked "did we build `AppShell`, `LeftRail`, `Inspector`, `StatusBar` to spec?" The feature-porting gap was never explicitly tracked.

---

### What's actually missing from OPEN_TASKS.md

None of these gaps appear there. The three `partial` rows (B01/B02/B03) are about `PlanCamera`/`SnapEngine` adoption, which is an internal wiring question. The authoring workbenches, collision readout, advisor, 3D layers, sheet canvas — these are user-facing features that existed before and have no representation in the current task list.

---

### Recommended next priorities

1. **Collision readout** — wire `collaborationConflictQueue` into the redesigned StatusBar (the infrastructure is complete, it just needs to be plugged in)
2. **3D layer visibility panel** — state fully exists (`viewerCategoryHidden`, `toggleViewerCategoryHidden`), just needs a UI surface
3. **Advisor panel** — swap the `AgentReviewModeShell` placeholder for `AdvisorPanel.tsx` + building preset selector
4. **Authoring workbenches** — the six panels are the biggest authoring gap; they need to be discoverable from the redesigned Inspector or a dedicated mode

Want me to add these to OPEN_TASKS.md and start on any of them?

input.new_string:
## Feature parity gaps — old Workspace vs. redesign

These features existed in `Workspace.tsx` (now at `/legacy`) and are not yet wired into `RedesignedWorkspace.tsx`. The §5 parity dashboard did not track these because the WP-UI work packages only measured whether the *new chrome components* were built, not whether every old-workspace feature was ported.

---

### T-10 · Collaboration conflict queue readout

**WP target:** WP-UI-A06 (StatusBar) · **Source:** `Workspace.tsx` lines 1019–1071 · **Status:** `open`

The collision infrastructure is complete but dark in the redesign. `packages/web/src/lib/collaborationConflictQueue.ts` detects `merge_id_collision` events from the WebSocket; `collaborationConflictStatus.ts` formats them. `RedesignedWorkspace.tsx` never subscribes to the conflict queue, so merge conflicts surface only as a generic `seedError`.

**Next moves:**
- Subscribe to `collaborationConflictQueueV1` in `RedesignedWorkspace.tsx` (mirror old lines 185–186, 337–347, 459–465)
- Add a conflict pill / expandable row to `StatusBar.tsx` that shows `inspectionReadout`, `retryAdvice`, `reason`, `blockingCommandType`
- Add vitest for the new StatusBar conflict slot
- Update §5 + §28 WP-UI-A06 when done

---

### T-11 · 3D layer visibility panel

**WP target:** WP-UI-B04/B08 (3D viewport) · **Source:** `Workspace.tsx` lines 1173–1240 · **Status:** `open`

The per-category visibility state is fully implemented (`viewerCategoryHidden`, `toggleViewerCategoryHidden`, `viewerClipElevMm`, `viewerClipFloorElevMm`, `setViewerClipElevMm`, `setViewerClipFloorElevMm` in the store) but has no UI surface in the redesign. The old workspace had a "3D layers" panel exposing category toggles (wall/floor/roof/stair/door/window/room) and section-box clip elevation sliders.

**Next moves:**
- Author a `Viewport3DLayersPanel.tsx` component (or add a collapsible section to Inspector) with per-category toggle rows + clip-elevation numeric inputs
- Wire into `RedesignedWorkspace.tsx` as a side panel or Inspector tab when the active view is 3D/Plan+3D
- Vitest for panel render + dispatch
- Update §5 + §28 WP-UI-B04/B08 when done

---

### T-12 · Advisor panel

**WP target:** WP-UI-E01–E04 (agent/advisor) · **Source:** `packages/web/src/advisor/AdvisorPanel.tsx` · **Status:** `open`

`AgentReviewModeShell` in `workspace/ModeShells.tsx` is a placeholder. The full `AdvisorPanel.tsx` exists and was wired in the old workspace (lines 1862–1872) — it receives `violations`, `buildingPreset`, `setBuildingPreset` and surfaces code-check results with actionable suggestions.

**Next moves:**
- Swap `AgentReviewModeShell` placeholder for `<AdvisorPanel>` wired to `useBimStore` (`violations`, `buildingPreset`, `setBuildingPreset`)
- Add building-preset selector to TopBar or Inspector when mode = Agent
- Vitest smoke for advisor mount + preset dispatch
- Update §5 + §28 WP-UI-E rows when done

---

### T-13 · Authoring workbenches

**WP target:** WP-UI-C (tools) · **Source:** `Workspace.tsx` lines 1812–1860 · **Status:** `open`

Six specialized authoring panels exist in the codebase but are not mounted in the redesign:

| Panel | File | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| Room separation | `workspace/RoomSeparationAuthoringWorkbench.tsx` | Draw/edit room separation lines |
| Level datum stack | `workspace/LevelDatumStackWorkbench.tsx` | Level elevation constraints + datum chains |
| Roof authoring | `workspace/RoofAuthoringWorkbench.tsx` | Roof footprint, slopes, overhangs |
| Material layer stack | `workspace/MaterialLayerStackWorkbench.tsx` | Wall/floor/roof layer + material assignment |
| Site authoring | `workspace/SiteAuthoringPanel.tsx` | Project settings, site boundary, north arrow |
| Room color scheme | `workspace/RoomColorSchemePanel.tsx` | Per-room color schemes by department/finish |

**Next moves:**
- Decide surface: context-sensitive Inspector tab (activates when the right element kind is selected) or dedicated mode-shell panels
- Mount each panel in `RedesignedWorkspace.tsx` wired to the same store props as old `Workspace.tsx`
- Vitest smoke for each mount
- Update §5 + §28 WP-UI-C rows when done

---

### T-14 · Plan view graphics matrix + saved-view overrides

**WP target:** WP-UI-B01 (drafting visuals) · **Source:** `workspace/PlanViewGraphicsMatrix.tsx`, `SavedViewTagGraphicsAuthoring.tsx`, `SavedViewTemplateGraphicsAuthoring.tsx` · **Status:** `open`

Per-category line weight / pattern / color overrides per plan view exist in the old workspace but have no UI in the redesign. Users cannot currently inspect or change how categories render per view.

**Next moves:**
- Mount `PlanViewGraphicsMatrix.tsx` in the redesign (likely as an Inspector tab or settings panel when a plan view is active)
- Mount `SavedViewTagGraphicsAuthoring` and `SavedViewTemplateGraphicsAuthoring` for per-view template overrides
- Wire to `activePlanViewId` + `planPresentationPreset` from store
- Update §5 + §28 WP-UI-B01 when done

---

### T-15 · Sheet canvas + section pane

**WP target:** WP-UI-A12 (view tabs) · **Source:** `workspace/SheetCanvas.tsx`, `workspace/SectionPlaceholderPane.tsx` · **Status:** `open`

Sheet mode and Section mode have placeholder shells in the redesign (`SheetModeShell`, `SectionModeShell` in `workspace/ModeShells.tsx`). The actual rendering components exist:
- `SheetCanvas.tsx` — titleblock, placed views, viewports
- `SectionPlaceholderPane.tsx` — section/elevation view

**Next moves:**
- Swap `SheetModeShell` placeholder for `<SheetCanvas>` wired to the active sheet id from `tabsModel`
- Swap `SectionModeShell` placeholder for `<SectionPlaceholderPane>`
- Vitest smoke for canvas mount
- Update §5 + §28 WP-UI-A12 when done

---

### T-16 · Collaboration presence + comments

**WP target:** WP-UI-A (app shell) · **Source:** `Workspace.tsx` lines 987–995, 1896–1960 · **Status:** `open`

User presence (peer avatars, `presencePeers`, `userDisplayName`, `userId`) and the comment thread UI are wired in the old workspace but absent from the redesign. The store keys exist (`comments`, `mergeComment`, `setComments`, `presencePeers`, `setPresencePeers`, `setIdentity`).

**Next moves:**
- Add peer-avatar row to TopBar right region (wired to `presencePeers`)
- Mount comments panel (sidebar or popover) in `RedesignedWorkspace.tsx`
- Wire identity (`userDisplayName`, `userId`) into TopBar avatar tile
- Vitest for presence + comment render
- Update §5 when done

---

## How to add a new task

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Open tasks — BIM AI UI

Living to-do for the redesigned UI. Anything that's *still open* lives here so it doesn't get buried in commit messages or a 1200-line spec. When a task closes, move it to spec/ui-ux-redesign-v1-spec.md §6 (Sprint Ledger) and delete its row here.

**Counterpart docs**:
- `spec/ui-ux-redesign-v1-spec.md` §32 — fuller-context visual-fidelity audit (V01–V15).
- `spec/ui-ux-redesign-v1-spec.md` §28 — WP-UI-* table (every row is `partial`; this file enumerates the *concrete next moves*, not the WP roll-up).
- `spec/ui-ux-redesign-v1-spec.md` §6 — sprint ledger of what already closed.

Last updated: 2026-05-06.

---

## High-impact (user-visible, blocks daily use)

---

## Medium (deferred from earlier WPs, called out in commits but not in spec)

---

## §28 WP-UI rows — table-level audit

40 of 43 rows are now `done` (2026-05-06 sweep). Three rows remain `partial`:

- **WP-UI-B01** (2D Plan canvas — drafting visuals): `planCanvasState.draftingPaintFor` not directly used in PlanCanvas.tsx; canvas achieves token-driven paint via symbology.ts but scale-dependent line weights and hatch visibility are not yet wired.
- **WP-UI-B02** (2D Plan canvas — pointer + snap grammar): `planCanvasState.classifyPointerStart` not used; PlanCanvas has its own pointer classification.
- **WP-UI-B03** (2D Plan canvas — zoom/pan/level/empty state): `planCanvasState.PlanCamera` not used; PlanCanvas has its own zoom/pan/camera. Anchor-toward-cursor zoom and strict 1:5–1:5000 bounds not applied.

Next sweep: wire `PlanCamera`, `SnapEngine`, and `draftingPaintFor` into `PlanCanvas.tsx` to close B01–B03.

---

## How to add a new task

1. Append a new `### T-NN · Title` heading at the end of the relevant section.
2. Fill: WP target / Source / Status, then a 1-paragraph "what" + a "Next moves" sub-list.
3. If it's user-visible, also add a row in spec §32. Otherwise just here.
4. Once closed, **delete the heading from this file** and add a row in spec §6 (Sprint Ledger) with the closing commit.

lines: +## Feature parity gaps — old Workspace vs. redesign

+These features existed in `Workspace.tsx` (now at `/legacy`) and are not yet wired into `RedesignedWorkspace.tsx`. The §5 parity dashboard did not track these because the WP-UI work packages only measured whether the *new chrome components* were built, not whether every old-workspace feature was ported.

+The collision infrastructure is complete but dark in the redesign. `packages/web/src/lib/collaborationConflictQueue.ts` detects `merge_id_collision` events from the WebSocket; `collaborationConflictStatus.ts` formats them. `RedesignedWorkspace.tsx` never subscribes to the conflict queue, so merge conflicts surface only as a generic `seedError`.
+- Add a conflict pill / expandable row to `StatusBar.tsx` that shows `inspectionReadout`, `retryAdvice`, `reason`, `blockingCommandType`
+- Add vitest for the new StatusBar conflict slot

+The per-category visibility state is fully implemented (`viewerCategoryHidden`, `toggleViewerCategoryHidden`, `viewerClipElevMm`, `viewerClipFloorElevMm`, `setViewerClipElevMm`, `setViewerClipFloorElevMm` in the store) but has no UI surface in the redesign. The old workspace had a "3D layers" panel exposing category toggles (wall/floor/roof/stair/door/window/room) and section-box clip elevation sliders.
+- Author a `Viewport3DLayersPanel.tsx` component (or add a collapsible section to Inspector) with per-category toggle rows + clip-elevation numeric inputs
+- Wire into `RedesignedWorkspace.tsx` as a side panel or Inspector tab when the active view is 3D/Plan+3D

+`AgentReviewModeShell` in `workspace/ModeShells.tsx` is a placeholder. The full `AdvisorPanel.tsx` exists and was wired in the old workspace (lines 1862–1872) — it receives `violations`, `buildingPreset`, `setBuildingPreset` and surfaces code-check results with actionable suggestions.
+- Add building-preset selector to TopBar or Inspector when mode = Agent
+- Vitest smoke for advisor mount + preset dispatch
+Six specialized authoring panels exist in the codebase but are not mounted in the redesign:
+| Room separation | `workspace/RoomSeparationAuthoringWorkbench.tsx` | Draw/edit room separation lines |
+| Level datum stack | `workspace/LevelDatumStackWorkbench.tsx` | Level elevation constraints + datum chains |
+| Roof authoring | `workspace/RoofAuthoringWorkbench.tsx` | Roof footprint, slopes, overhangs |
+| Material layer stack | `workspace/MaterialLayerStackWorkbench.tsx` | Wall/floor/roof layer + material assignment |
+| Site authoring | `workspace/SiteAuthoringPanel.tsx` | Project settings, site boundary, north arrow |
+| Room color scheme | `workspace/RoomColorSchemePanel.tsx` | Per-room color schemes by department/finish |
+- Decide surface: context-sensitive Inspector tab (activates when the right element kind is selected) or dedicated mode-shell panels
+- Mount each panel in `RedesignedWorkspace.tsx` wired to the same store props as old `Workspace.tsx`
+### T-14 · Plan view graphics matrix + saved-view overrides
+Per-category line weight / pattern / color overrides per plan view exist in the old workspace but have no UI in the redesign. Users cannot currently inspect or change how categories render per view.
+- Mount `PlanViewGraphicsMatrix.tsx` in the redesign (likely as an Inspector tab or settings panel when a plan view is active)
+- Mount `SavedViewTagGraphicsAuthoring` and `SavedViewTemplateGraphicsAuthoring` for per-view template overrides
+Sheet mode and Section mode have placeholder shells in the redesign (`SheetModeShell`, `SectionModeShell` in `workspace/ModeShells.tsx`). The actual rendering components exist:
+- Swap `SheetModeShell` placeholder for `<SheetCanvas>` wired to the active sheet id from `tabsModel`

+User presence (peer avatars, `presencePeers`, `userDisplayName`, `userId`) and the comment thread UI are wired in the old workspace but absent from the redesign. The store keys exist (`comments`, `mergeComment`, `setComments`, `presencePeers`, `setPresencePeers`, `setIdentity`).
+- Add peer-avatar row to TopBar right region (wired to `presencePeers`)
+- Mount comments panel (sidebar or popover) in `RedesignedWorkspace.tsx`
+- Wire identity (`userDisplayName`, `userId`) into TopBar avatar tile

content.text:
Good. Now the spawn prompt — here's one you can copy and swap the T-number on:

---

```
You are working on the BIM AI project at /Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai.
It is a browser-based BIM tool (Revit alternative). Stack: React 19 + Vite 6 + TypeScript,
Tailwind, zustand store, vitest, Playwright.

Read OPEN_TASKS.md and implement **T-10** fully.

Rules:
- Only commit when the task is done. Commit message format: `feat(ui): T-10 <short title>`
  followed by `Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>`
- Push to origin/main when done.
- After committing: delete T-10's heading from OPEN_TASKS.md and add a row to
  spec/ui-ux-redesign-v1-spec.md §6 sprint ledger (same table format as existing rows).
- Run `pnpm --filter web typecheck` and `pnpm --filter web test run` before committing;
  fix any failures you introduced.
- Do not touch unrelated files. Another agent may be working in parallel.
- The legacy implementation to port from is in packages/web/src/Workspace.tsx —
  search for the relevant section noted in the task's "Source" field.
```

Swap `T-10` for whichever task you want the agent to pick up. The task's "Next moves" list in OPEN_TASKS.md gives it the specific sub-steps, and the "Source" field tells it exactly which lines of the old `Workspace.tsx` to reference.

1	/**
2	 * Empty / loading / error states — spec §25.
3	 *
4	 * Canonical content (headline, hint, primary CTA, severity) per
5	 * documented surface. The corresponding React renderer composes a
6	 * StateBlock against this catalogue.
7	 */
8	
9	export type UiStateKind =
10	  | 'canvas-empty'
11	  | 'canvas-loading'
12	  | 'network-offline'
13	  | 'engine-error'
14	  | 'conflict-409'
15	  | 'permission-denied';
16	
17	export type UiStateSeverity = 'info' | 'warning' | 'danger';
18	
19	export interface UiStatePattern {
20	  kind: UiStateKind;
21	  severity: UiStateSeverity;
22	  headline: string;
23	  hint: string;
24	  cta?: { label: string; intent: 'primary' | 'secondary'; action: string };
25	  /** Aria-live politeness expected for this state. */
26	  ariaLive: 'polite' | 'assertive' | 'off';
27	}
28	
29	export const UI_STATE_PATTERNS: Record<UiStateKind, UiStatePattern> = {
30	  'canvas-empty': {
31	    kind: 'canvas-empty',
32	    severity: 'info',
33	    headline: 'This level is empty.',
34	    hint: 'Press W to draw a wall, click "Insert seed house" below, or use the project menu (top-left BIM AI seed ▾).',
35	    cta: { label: 'Insert seed house', intent: 'primary', action: 'project.insert-seed' },
36	    ariaLive: 'off',
37	  },
38	  'canvas-loading': {
39	    kind: 'canvas-loading',
40	    severity: 'info',
41	    headline: 'Loading model…',
42	    hint: 'Streaming geometry from the engine.',
43	    ariaLive: 'polite',
44	  },
45	  'network-offline': {
46	    kind: 'network-offline',
47	    severity: 'warning',
48	    headline: 'Network connection lost.',
49	    hint: 'Local edits remain queued; we will resync as soon as you are back online.',
50	    cta: { label: 'Retry', intent: 'secondary', action: 'network.retry' },
51	    ariaLive: 'assertive',
52	  },
53	  'engine-error': {
54	    kind: 'engine-error',
55	    severity: 'danger',
56	    headline: 'The engine returned an error.',
57	    hint: 'Copy the diagnostics or reload to try again. Your local changes are preserved.',
58	    cta: { label: 'Copy diagnostics', intent: 'secondary', action: 'engine.copy-diagnostics' },
59	    ariaLive: 'assertive',
60	  },
61	  'conflict-409': {
62	    kind: 'conflict-409',
63	    severity: 'warning',
64	    headline: 'Server-side conflict detected.',
65	    hint: 'A newer revision exists. Reload to continue editing.',
66	    cta: { label: 'Reload', intent: 'primary', action: 'document.reload' },
67	    ariaLive: 'assertive',
68	  },
69	  'permission-denied': {
70	    kind: 'permission-denied',
71	    severity: 'danger',
72	    headline: 'This project is read-only.',
73	    hint: 'Save a copy to make changes.',
74	    cta: { label: 'Save a copy', intent: 'primary', action: 'document.save-copy' },
75	    ariaLive: 'assertive',
76	  },
77	};
78	
79	export function patternFor(kind: UiStateKind): UiStatePattern {
80	  return UI_STATE_PATTERNS[kind];
81	}

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(ui): show "Loading…" instead of "This level is empty" during initial model fetch

On mount, insertSeedHouse() fires immediately (seedLoading = true) while
elementsById is still empty, so the empty-state overlay appeared with
"This level is empty." before the model had a chance to load.

Switch patternFor to canvas-loading while seedLoading is true so the
overlay reads "Loading model… Streaming geometry from the engine." and
drops the CTA button. Reverts to canvas-empty (with seed-house CTA) only
once the fetch settles and the model is genuinely empty.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

content.text:
Pushed. Now for your rendering question.

---

That image is a **Revit architectural visualization** render — specifically a hidden-line / shaded mode with:

- **Real geometry per element type**: door frames with proper jamb depth, window glazing panes set into reveals, railing balusters as individual members, stair treads as solid geometry, and roof with a true gable profile (not a box proxy)
- **Directional shadows** cast onto the site slab
- **Ambient occlusion** in concave joins (eaves, reveals, wall/floor junctions)
- **Surface materials** with subtle differentiation — cladding vs. white render vs. glazing vs. metal railing
- **Edge lines** from a dedicated hidden-line pass (not just geometry edges)

Here's exactly what's between us and that today:

**Geometry gaps (biggest delta)**

| Element | Current app | What's needed |
|---|---|---|
| Doors | Box proxy in wall opening | Frame + panel geometry with correct jamb depth |
| Windows | Box proxy | Glazing pane + frame reveal |
| Railings | Line path (just added) | Balusters + rail cap as extruded geometry |
| Stairs | Box proxy | Tread/riser solid geometry |
| Roof | Box proxy | True hip/gable mesh from footprint + pitch |
| Site | Flat plane | Slab with thickness |
| Walls | Extruded rectangles | Correct (already close) |

**Rendering pipeline gaps**

| Feature | Current | Needed |
|---|---|---|
| Shadow casting | None | Three.js `DirectionalLight` shadow map (already partially wired in materials.ts, not enabled in scene) |
| Ambient occlusion | None | SSAO post-pass (Three.js `SSAOPass`) or baked AO from geometry |
| Edge lines | None | `EdgesGeometry` overlay already in code for selection; needs a separate render pass for all geometry |
| Material differentiation | Flat colors per category | Roughness/metalness variation per surface type (glazing = 0 roughness, metal railing = 0.5) |

**Rough effort order**: Real door/window/railing geometry → enable shadow map → edge-line pass → AO. Each step is independent and meaningful on its own. The shadow map is probably the single highest visual-impact/effort ratio — Three.js `DirectionalLight.castShadow = true` with a 2048² map would give you the cast shadows on the site slab immediately, and the wiring in `materials.ts` already defines the sun light parameters.

Want me to start with the shadow map + edge lines since those'd be visible immediately without new geometry?

input.description: Deep audit of 3D rendering pipeline for V2 spec
input.prompt:
I'm writing a detailed V2 rendering specification for a BIM web application. I need you to deeply audit the current 3D rendering code so I can describe exactly what exists today and what needs to be built. The goal is to achieve Revit-quality architectural visualization (hidden-line + shaded, directional shadows, AO, edge lines, real element geometry).

Please read these files fully and report back with very specific implementation details:

1. `packages/web/src/Viewport.tsx` — the main Three.js renderer. I need to know:
   - Exactly how each element kind (wall, floor, door, window, stair, railing, roof, site, room) is turned into Three.js geometry today (function names, geometry type, dimensions used)
   - How the scene is lit (light types, positions, shadow settings)
   - Whether EdgesGeometry/line passes exist
   - Whether shadow maps are enabled
   - The render loop / animation frame setup

2. `packages/web/src/viewport/materials.ts` — I need to know:
   - What `resolveLighting()` returns exactly (light types, color, intensity, position, shadow settings)
   - What `resolveCategoryMaterial()` returns (roughness, metalness, color source)
   - Whether there's any AO, shadow, or post-processing setup

3. `packages/web/src/viewport/cameraRig.ts` — field of view, near/far clip planes if set there

4. Look for any existing `SSAOPass`, `EffectComposer`, `OutlinePass`, or post-processing imports anywhere in the web package

5. Look at what element kinds are defined in `@bim-ai/core` (check `packages/core/src/` or wherever Element types are defined) — specifically wall, door, window, stair, railing, roof, floor, site fields like `heightMm`, `widthMm`, `thicknessMm`, `footprintMm`, `pathMm`, `pitchDeg`, `overhangMm`, etc.

Report all findings with specific file:line references. I need enough detail to write precise work packages.

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me compile a comprehensive audit report:

## Detailed V2 Rendering Specification Audit

I've conducted a thorough analysis of the current 3D rendering implementation. Here are the exact findings for your V2 specification work:

---

### 1. VIEWPORT.TSX — GEOMETRY CREATION BY ELEMENT KIND

**File:** `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/web/src/Viewport.tsx`

Each element kind is converted to Three.js geometry as follows:

#### **Wall** (lines 318-347)
- **Function:** `makeWallMesh(wall, elevM, paint, selectedId?)`
- **Geometry:** `THREE.BoxGeometry(len, height, thick)`
- **Dimensions used:**
  - `len` = `Math.hypot(end.xMm - start.xMm, end.yMm - start.yMm) / 1000` (wall run length in meters)
  - `height` = `clamp(wall.heightMm / 1000, 0.25, 40)` (clamped 0.25–40m)
  - `thick` = `clamp(wall.thicknessMm / 1000, 0.05, 2)` (clamped 0.05–2m)
- **Material:** `MeshStandardMaterial`
- **Positioning:** Centered at `(sx + dx/2, elevM + height/2, sz + dz/2)` with rotation `y = atan2(dz, dx)`
- **Selection color:** Uses `paint?.selection.selectedColor ?? '#fb923c'` when selected

#### **Floor** (lines 149-176)
- **Function:** `makeFloorSlabMesh(floor, elementsById, paint)`
- **Geometry:** `THREE.BoxGeometry(spanX/1000, thickness, spanZ/1000)`
- **Dimensions used:**
  - `spanX`, `spanZ` = bounds of `floor.boundaryMm` (XZ polygon)
  - `thickness` = `clamp(floor.thicknessMm / 1000, 0.05, 1.8)` (clamped 0.05–1.8m)
  - `elevation` = looked up from level via `elevationMForLevel(floor.levelId)`
- **Material:** `MeshStandardMaterial` with `opacity: 0.92, transparent: true`
- **Positioning:** Centered at `(boundsCx/1000, elev + th/2, boundsCz/1000)`

#### **Door** (lines 349-373)
- **Function:** `makeDoorMesh(door, wall, elevM, paint, selectedId?)`
- **Geometry:** `THREE.BoxGeometry(width, height, depth)`
- **Dimensions used:**
  - `width` = `clamp(door.widthMm / 1000, 0.35, 4)` (clamped 0.35–4m)
  - `height` = `clamp(wall.heightMm / 1000 * 0.86, 0.6, 2.2)` (86% of wall height, clamped 0.6–2.2m)
  - `depth` = `clamp(wall.thicknessMm / 1000 + 0.08, 0.08, 2)` (wall thickness + 80mm reveal, clamped)
  - Position calculated via `hostedXZ(door, wall)` using `door.alongT` parameter (0–1 along wall length)
- **Positioning:** Rotated to `wallYaw(wall)` orientation

#### **Window** (lines 375-402)
- **Function:** `makeWindowMesh(win, wall, elevM, paint, selectedId?)`
- **Geometry:** `THREE.BoxGeometry(width, height, depth)`
- **Dimensions used:**
  - `width` = `clamp(win.widthMm / 1000, 0.14, 4)` (clamped 0.14–4m)
  - `sill` = `clamp(win.sillHeightMm / 1000, 0.06, wall.heightMm/1000 - 0.08)` (sill height from Element)
  - `height` = `clamp(win.heightMm / 1000, 0.05, wall.heightMm - sill - 0.06)` (clamped window height)
  - `depth` = `clamp(wall.thicknessMm / 1000 + 0.02, 0.06, 1.5)` (wall thickness + 20mm reveal)
- **Material:** `MeshStandardMaterial` with `opacity: 0.84, transparent: true`

#### **Stair** (lines 263-316)
- **Function:** `makeStairVolumeMesh(stair, elementsById, paint, selectedId?)`
- **Geometry:** `THREE.BoxGeometry(len, rise, width)` (bounding volume proxy)
- **Dimensions used:**
  - `len` = `Math.hypot(runEnd.xMm - runStart.xMm, runEnd.yMm - runStart.yMm) / 1000` (run diagonal)
  - `width` = `clamp(stair.widthMm / 1000, 0.3, 4)` (clamped 0.3–4m)
  - `rise` = computed from level elevations: `abs(topLevelElev - baseLevelElev) / 1000` or fallback to `stair.riserMm * 16 / 1000`
  - `rise` clamped to `(0.5, 12)` meters
- **Positioning:** Centered at midpoint of run line with `rotation.y = atan2(dx, dz)` (projected)

#### **Railing** (lines 421-478)
- **Function:** `makeRailingMesh(railing, elementsById, paint)`
- **Geometry:** `THREE.Group` containing multiple `THREE.Mesh` boxes for rail segments
- **Dimensions used:**
  - `guardHeight` = `clamp(railing.guardHeightMm ?? 1050 / 1000, 0.5, 2.2)` (default 1050mm)
  - Rail segments built from `railing.pathMm` (XY polygon):
    - Each segment: `BoxGeometry(railLen, 0.05, 0.05)` where `railLen = sqrt(planSeg² + riseY²)`
    - `riseY` = interpolated based on path progress between base and top level elevations
- **Material:** `MeshStandardMaterial` with optional roughness/metalness from paint bundle

#### **Roof** (lines 178-261)
- **Function:** `makeRoofMassMesh(roof, elementsById, paint)`
- **Geometry:** Custom `BufferGeometry` with hand-computed positions (gable-pitched rectangle proxy)
- **Dimensions used:**
  - `footprint` = `roof.footprintMm` (XY polygon boundary)
  - `overhang` = `clamp(roof.overhangMm ?? 0 / 1000, 0, 5)` (clamped 0–5m)
  - `slope` = `clamp(roof.slopeDeg ?? 25, 5, 70)` degrees (clamped 5–70°, default 25°)
  - `ridgeY = eaveY + halfSpan * tan(slopeRad)` (eave height + height rise from slope)
  - Eave height determined from tallest wall at reference level
  - **Geometry construction:** Two pitched slopes (north/south or east/west depending on footprint aspect ratio) + 2 gable end faces
  - Positions array hard-coded per ridge orientation (lines 212-242)
- **Material:** `MeshStandardMaterial` with `opacity: 0.94, side: THREE.DoubleSide`

#### **Room** (lines 404-419)
- **Function:** `makeRoomRibbon(room, elevM, paint)`
- **Geometry:** `THREE.LineLoop` (1D line polygon)
- **Dimensions used:**
  - Points from `room.outlineMm` (XY polygon)
  - Elevated at `elevM + 0.035` (3.5cm above floor for visibility)
- **Rendering:** Wire outline only, no fill

#### **Site** (lines 480-517)
- **Function:** `makeSiteMesh(site, elementsById, paint)`
- **Geometry:** `THREE.ExtrudeGeometry` (2D boundary extruded vertically)
- **Dimensions used:**
  - Boundary from `site.boundaryMm` (XY polygon)
  - `padThickness` = `clamp(site.padThicknessMm ?? 150 / 1000, 0.05, 2)` (default 150mm, clamped 50–2000mm)
  - `baseOffset` = `site.baseOffsetMm ?? 0 / 1000` (vertical offset from reference level)
  - Geometry rotated -π/2 around X to lie in XZ plane
- **Material:** `MeshStandardMaterial` with `opacity: 0.85, transparent: true`

---

### 2. LIGHTING CONFIGURATION

**File:** `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/web/src/viewport/materials.ts` (lines 152–169)

#### **`resolveLighting()` Returns:**
```javascript
{
  sun: {
    intensity: 0.85,
    azimuthDeg: 145,
    elevationDeg: 35,          // Fixed 35° elevation per spec
    color: '#fff8ec',          // Warm white
    shadowMapSize: 2048        // Defined but NOT used
  },
  hemi: {
    intensity: 0.45,
    skyColor: '#d3e2ff',       // Cool blue sky
    groundColor: '#d8d3c4'     // Warm beige ground
  }
}
```

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me compile a comprehensive audit report:

## Detailed V2 Rendering Specification Audit

I've conducted a thorough analysis of the current 3D rendering implementation. Here are the exact findings for your V2 specification work:

---

### 1. VIEWPORT.TSX — GEOMETRY CREATION BY ELEMENT KIND

**File:** `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/web/src/Viewport.tsx`

Each element kind is converted to Three.js geometry as follows:

#### **Wall** (lines 318-347)
- **Function:** `makeWallMesh(wall, elevM, paint, selectedId?)`
- **Geometry:** `THREE.BoxGeometry(len, height, thick)`
- **Dimensions used:**
  - `len` = `Math.hypot(end.xMm - start.xMm, end.yMm - start.yMm) / 1000` (wall run length in meters)
  - `height` = `clamp(wall.heightMm / 1000, 0.25, 40)` (clamped 0.25–40m)
  - `thick` = `clamp(wall.thicknessMm / 1000, 0.05, 2)` (clamped 0.05–2m)
- **Material:** `MeshStandardMaterial`
- **Positioning:** Centered at `(sx + dx/2, elevM + height/2, sz + dz/2)` with rotation `y = atan2(dz, dx)`
- **Selection color:** Uses `paint?.selection.selectedColor ?? '#fb923c'` when selected

#### **Floor** (lines 149-176)
- **Function:** `makeFloorSlabMesh(floor, elementsById, paint)`
- **Geometry:** `THREE.BoxGeometry(spanX/1000, thickness, spanZ/1000)`
- **Dimensions used:**
  - `spanX`, `spanZ` = bounds of `floor.boundaryMm` (XZ polygon)
  - `thickness` = `clamp(floor.thicknessMm / 1000, 0.05, 1.8)` (clamped 0.05–1.8m)
  - `elevation` = looked up from level via `elevationMForLevel(floor.levelId)`
- **Material:** `MeshStandardMaterial` with `opacity: 0.92, transparent: true`
- **Positioning:** Centered at `(boundsCx/1000, elev + th/2, boundsCz/1000)`

#### **Door** (lines 349-373)
- **Function:** `makeDoorMesh(door, wall, elevM, paint, selectedId?)`
- **Geometry:** `THREE.BoxGeometry(width, height, depth)`
- **Dimensions used:**
  - `width` = `clamp(door.widthMm / 1000, 0.35, 4)` (clamped 0.35–4m)
  - `height` = `clamp(wall.heightMm / 1000 * 0.86, 0.6, 2.2)` (86% of wall height, clamped 0.6–2.2m)
  - `depth` = `clamp(wall.thicknessMm / 1000 + 0.08, 0.08, 2)` (wall thickness + 80mm reveal, clamped)
  - Position calculated via `hostedXZ(door, wall)` using `door.alongT` parameter (0–1 along wall length)
- **Positioning:** Rotated to `wallYaw(wall)` orientation

#### **Window** (lines 375-402)
- **Function:** `makeWindowMesh(win, wall, elevM, paint, selectedId?)`
- **Geometry:** `THREE.BoxGeometry(width, height, depth)`
- **Dimensions used:**
  - `width` = `clamp(win.widthMm / 1000, 0.14, 4)` (clamped 0.14–4m)
  - `sill` = `clamp(win.sillHeightMm / 1000, 0.06, wall.heightMm/1000 - 0.08)` (sill height from Element)
  - `height` = `clamp(win.heightMm / 1000, 0.05, wall.heightMm - sill - 0.06)` (clamped window height)
  - `depth` = `clamp(wall.thicknessMm / 1000 + 0.02, 0.06, 1.5)` (wall thickness + 20mm reveal)
- **Material:** `MeshStandardMaterial` with `opacity: 0.84, transparent: true`

#### **Stair** (lines 263-316)
- **Function:** `makeStairVolumeMesh(stair, elementsById, paint, selectedId?)`
- **Geometry:** `THREE.BoxGeometry(len, rise, width)` (bounding volume proxy)
- **Dimensions used:**
  - `len` = `Math.hypot(runEnd.xMm - runStart.xMm, runEnd.yMm - runStart.yMm) / 1000` (run diagonal)
  - `width` = `clamp(stair.widthMm / 1000, 0.3, 4)` (clamped 0.3–4m)
  - `rise` = computed from level elevations: `abs(topLevelElev - baseLevelElev) / 1000` or fallback to `stair.riserMm * 16 / 1000`
  - `rise` clamped to `(0.5, 12)` meters
- **Positioning:** Centered at midpoint of run line with `rotation.y = atan2(dx, dz)` (projected)

#### **Railing** (lines 421-478)
- **Function:** `makeRailingMesh(railing, elementsById, paint)`
- **Geometry:** `THREE.Group` containing multiple `THREE.Mesh` boxes for rail segments
- **Dimensions used:**
  - `guardHeight` = `clamp(railing.guardHeightMm ?? 1050 / 1000, 0.5, 2.2)` (default 1050mm)
  - Rail segments built from `railing.pathMm` (XY polygon):
    - Each segment: `BoxGeometry(railLen, 0.05, 0.05)` where `railLen = sqrt(planSeg² + riseY²)`
    - `riseY` = interpolated based on path progress between base and top level elevations
- **Material:** `MeshStandardMaterial` with optional roughness/metalness from paint bundle

#### **Roof** (lines 178-261)
- **Function:** `makeRoofMassMesh(roof, elementsById, paint)`
- **Geometry:** Custom `BufferGeometry` with hand-computed positions (gable-pitched rectangle proxy)
- **Dimensions used:**
  - `footprint` = `roof.footprintMm` (XY polygon boundary)
  - `overhang` = `clamp(roof.overhangMm ?? 0 / 1000, 0, 5)` (clamped 0–5m)
  - `slope` = `clamp(roof.slopeDeg ?? 25, 5, 70)` degrees (clamped 5–70°, default 25°)
  - `ridgeY = eaveY + halfSpan * tan(slopeRad)` (eave height + height rise from slope)
  - Eave height determined from tallest wall at reference level
  - **Geometry construction:** Two pitched slopes (north/south or east/west depending on footprint aspect ratio) + 2 gable end faces
  - Positions array hard-coded per ridge orientation (lines 212-242)
- **Material:** `MeshStandardMaterial` with `opacity: 0.94, side: THREE.DoubleSide`

#### **Room** (lines 404-419)
- **Function:** `makeRoomRibbon(room, elevM, paint)`
- **Geometry:** `THREE.LineLoop` (1D line polygon)
- **Dimensions used:**
  - Points from `room.outlineMm` (XY polygon)
  - Elevated at `elevM + 0.035` (3.5cm above floor for visibility)
- **Rendering:** Wire outline only, no fill

#### **Site** (lines 480-517)
- **Function:** `makeSiteMesh(site, elementsById, paint)`
- **Geometry:** `THREE.ExtrudeGeometry` (2D boundary extruded vertically)
- **Dimensions used:**
  - Boundary from `site.boundaryMm` (XY polygon)
  - `padThickness` = `clamp(site.padThicknessMm ?? 150 / 1000, 0.05, 2)` (default 150mm, clamped 50–2000mm)
  - `baseOffset` = `site.baseOffsetMm ?? 0 / 1000` (vertical offset from reference level)
  - Geometry rotated -π/2 around X to lie in XZ plane
- **Material:** `MeshStandardMaterial` with `opacity: 0.85, transparent: true`

lastPrompt: create a document defining everything that is a gap still, as workpackages. that will be version 2 then, once we can really render a house like that. be really specific, go into details. not shallow,…

content.content:
# Element Rendering Requirements

Reference for which element properties are required for each element to be
**visually present** in the 3D view, plan view, and section view. Gaps here
are the most common cause of "I added element X but it doesn't appear".

---

## Roof

### 3D view (glTF mesh)

The glTF exporter only emits a pitched roof mesh when **all** of the following
hold. Any miss causes the roof to be silently omitted from the 3D scene.

| Requirement | Value needed | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `roofGeometryMode` | `"gable_pitched_rectangle"` | Default `"mass_box"` produces **no mesh** — it is a plan/section proxy only |
| Footprint shape | Exactly 4 axis-aligned rectangle corners (≤1 mm tolerance) | L-shapes, hip polygons → deferred, no 3D mesh yet |
| `slopeDeg` | non-null float | If `None`, roof is skipped |
| `referenceLevelId` | resolves to a `LevelElem` | Broken ref → skipped |

Extension emitted when eligible: `bim_ai_gable_roof_v0`

**Unsupported footprint shapes in 3D (deferred):**
- Concave / L-shaped → `"valley_candidate_deferred"`
- Convex polygon with >4 corners (hip) → `"hip_candidate_deferred"`

### Section view

Roofs appear in section regardless of geometry mode, as long as the footprint
intersects the section cut's crop band (`cropDepthMm / 2` on each side of the
cut line).

- `roofGeometryMode = "gable_pitched_rectangle"` → renders with ridge line +
  sloped eave edges (`proxyKind: "gablePitchedRectangleChord"`)
- Any other mode → renders a flat footprint chord with a peak point
  (`proxyKind: "footprintChord"`)

**Key code:** `app/bim_ai/section_projection_primitives.py` —
`build_section_projection_primitives()`  
**Geometry decision matrix:** `app/bim_ai/roof_geometry.py` —
`roof_geometry_support_token_v0()`

---

## Section cut

A `section_cut` element creates a section tab in the UI. The section view
renders by calling `GET /api/models/{id}/projection/section/{sectionCutId}`.

For content to appear:

| Requirement | Notes |
|---|---|
| Section line must intersect elements | Elements must fall within `cropDepthMm / 2` of the cut line (perp distance) |
| `lineStartMm` / `lineEndMm` span must cover the building footprint | Short cut lines leave out geometry |
| `cropDepthMm` large enough | For a full-building section, use ≥ building depth + some margin |

The section marker/symbol (dashed line + arrows) renders on the plan canvas
