content.text:
Spec is at `spec/rendering-v2-spec.md`. Here's the honest distance summary:

---

**What gets you there, in order of visual impact:**

**Phase R1 — Pipeline (1–2 days total, no geometry changes):**
- **Shadow maps** (R1-01): `renderer.shadowMap.enabled = true` + configure `DirectionalLight.castShadow`. Every mesh gets `castShadow/receiveShadow`. Immediate and dramatic.
- **Edge lines** (R1-05): `EdgesGeometry` child on every mesh. The crisp outlines in the reference image come almost entirely from this.
- **SSAO** (R1-04): Via `SSAOPass`. Darkens wall/floor junctions and eave soffits. Subtle but makes the geometry read as solid.
- **Correct sun direction** (R1-02): Replace the hardcoded `(8, 12, 6)` position with azimuth/elevation math.

**Phase R2 — Geometry (biggest gap, ~2 weeks):**
- Doors, windows, railings, stairs are all box proxies today. Each needs a real multi-part mesh. Floor slabs use bounding box instead of the actual polygon. Roof mesh is partially correct for rectangles but breaks on L-shapes.

**Phase R3 — Materials (~3–4 days):**
- Glass needs roughness 0.05, env map reflection. Railing needs metalness 0.65. Cladding is geometry (individual boards) or a normal map.

**Phase R4 — Section perspective (~2–3 days):**
- The reference image is a section cut. That requires stencil-buffer plane capping — no interior faces exist on the current geometry, so without this the section just shows open boxes.

**Bottom line:** R1 alone (shadows + edges) would get you maybe 40% of the way to that image in a day or two. The remaining 60% is the geometry work in R2 — particularly door/window reveals, railing balusters, and the roof — which is the bulk of the effort.

message.content: how can i distribute Phase R1 for multiple agents to work in parallel? if you were CTO with a devteam, how could you distribute them?

content.text:
The honest answer is that R1 is **not as parallelisable as it looks** — three of the five WPs touch the same 50-line block in `Viewport.tsx`. Here's how I'd think about it as a CTO:

---

## Dependency graph first

```
R1-02 (sun direction)     ──┐
R1-01 (shadow map)        ──┤── all edit the renderer init
R1-03 (EffectComposer)    ──┘   block + the render loop

R1-04 (SSAO)              ── hard dep on R1-03 (needs composer to exist)

R1-05 (EdgesGeometry)     ── touches ONLY the mesh factory functions
                               (makeWallMesh, makeDoorMesh, etc.)
                               completely separate from the pipeline
```

R1-01, R1-02, R1-03 all write to the same ~50 lines: renderer construction, DirectionalLight setup, and `tick()`. If two agents touch those simultaneously you get a merge conflict on nearly every line. **The parallelism gain is eaten by the conflict resolution cost.**

---

## How I'd actually distribute it

**Agent A — "Renderer pipeline"**
Takes R1-01 + R1-02 + R1-03 as one unit. These belong together: shadow map enable, sun position math, and EffectComposer setup are all edits to the same renderer initialization block. Done in one commit. ~half a day.

**Agent B — "Edge lines"**
Takes R1-05 only. Writes the `addEdges()` helper and calls it from every factory function (`makeWallMesh`, `makeFloorSlabMesh`, `makeDoorMesh`, etc.). Zero overlap with Agent A — they're editing different functions. Can start immediately, merge independently. ~half a day.

**Agent C — "SSAO" (starts after A merges)**
Takes R1-04. Adds the `SSAOPass` after it can import the `composer` ref that A created. One hour of work — gated only by A landing.

---

## Timeline

```
Hour 0    Agent A starts: shadow + sun + composer
Hour 0    Agent B starts: addEdges() on all factories

Hour 4    Agent B merges (independent, no conflict)
Hour 5    Agent A merges
Hour 5    Agent C starts: SSAOPass (composer now exists)
Hour 6    Agent C merges

Total wall time: ~6 hours vs ~10 hours sequential
```

---

## The spawn prompts

**Agent A:**
```
Work in /Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai. Read spec/rendering-v2-spec.md
sections R1-01, R1-02, and R1-03 and implement all three as a single
commit. They must be done together — they all edit the renderer
initialization block in packages/web/src/Viewport.tsx and materials.ts.
Do not touch the mesh factory functions (makeWallMesh etc.) or add any
post-processing passes beyond RenderPass + OutputPass.
Run pnpm --filter web typecheck before committing.
Commit: "feat(3d): R1-01/02/03 — shadow map, sun direction, EffectComposer"
Push to origin/main when done.

Task: R1-01 + R1-02 + R1-03
```

**Agent B:**
```
Work in /Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai. Read spec/rendering-v2-spec.md
section R1-05 and implement it. Add the addEdges() helper function
in packages/web/src/Viewport.tsx and call it from every mesh factory
function (makeWallMesh, makeFloorSlabMesh, makeDoorMesh, makeWindowMesh,
makeStairVolumeMesh, makeRailingMesh, makeRoofMassMesh, makeSiteMesh).
Do NOT touch the renderer initialization, lighting setup, render loop,
or EffectComposer — another agent is working on those in parallel.
Run pnpm --filter web typecheck before committing.
Commit: "feat(3d): R1-05 — EdgesGeometry overlay on all element meshes"
Push to origin/main when done.

Task: R1-05
```

**Agent C (launch after A merges):**
```
Work in /Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai. Read spec/rendering-v2-spec.md
section R1-04. The EffectComposer is already set up (landed in a prior
commit). Add the SSAOPass to the existing composer chain in
packages/web/src/Viewport.tsx and add the SSAO parameter tokens to
packages/web/src/viewport/materials.ts.
Run pnpm --filter web typecheck before committing.
Commit: "feat(3d): R1-04 — SSAO pass"
Push to origin/main when done.

Task: R1-04
```

---

The only real risk is if A and B both pull from origin at the same time and one of the factory functions is edited by both. B only touches factory functions; A explicitly avoids them — so with those constraints in the prompts, the merge should be clean.
